content.claim: Cross-national OECD evidence attributes the two- to fourfold increase in autism spectrum disorder diagnoses over 15 years largely to improved detection and diagnosis, not underlying prevalence increases. This suggests diagnostic practice change is a major driver across comparable healthcare systems.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: Not Hungary-specific; Hungarian-specific incidence-versus-detection decomposition remains unavailable
Autism prevalence trends may not generalise to all special educational needs categories
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
